candidates.title: Emoji art-direction loop produced usable house artifacts
candidates.text: The room turned rough emoji references into a coherent Lobstar-house visual direction, selected four reaction artifacts, cleaned them to emoji size, and installed them as server culture.
candidates.description: Redacted from live Discord discussion. Keeps the lesson: community taste corrections can turn chat into durable artifacts.

candidates.title: Production correction catches bad crop and background gutters
candidates.text: A community member flags that the selected emoji artwork does not fit the frame and still shows unwanted background gutter, forcing a production cleanup pass before upload.
candidates.description: Redacted from live Discord discussion. Keeps the lesson: production-quality corrections should rank above more concept generation.

candidates.title: Student portrait needs more crustacean identity
candidates.text: The room likes the apprentice portrait direction, but corrects that it is too human and should read as a small crustacean apprentice with trumpet, papers, ink, and books.
candidates.description: Redacted from live Discord discussion. Keeps the lesson: identity art should preserve role and anatomy, not only style.

candidates.title: Brute-force shilling creates account-suspension risk
candidates.text: A member reports that multiple accounts were suspended while shilling and the room reframes the safer path as posting artifacts, clips, explanations, and workshop evidence instead of raw buy-pressure spam.
candidates.description: Redacted from live Discord discussion. Keeps the lesson: market attention should be converted into artifacts rather than spam behavior.

candidates.title: Market-uptrend prompt asks what is being cooked
candidates.text: A member asks whether anything is being prepared for the market uptrend and pushes for visible output rather than wasting attention.
candidates.description: Redacted from live Discord discussion. Keeps the lesson: timely market pressure can be useful, but needs an artifact plan to become signal.

candidates.title: Pure emoji reaction without context
candidates.text: A member posts only a custom emoji reaction with no explanation, artifact path, or next step.
candidates.description: Redacted from live Discord discussion. Keeps the lesson: emoji usage proves culture but a standalone reaction is not itself a ranked work item.
